title: Mattapan store owner pleads guilty in $6.7M SNAP fraud scheme
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Antonio Bonheur, 74, of Mattapan admitted to $6.7 million SNAP fraud at Jesula Variety Store between 2021-2025.
Fraudulent transactions involved exchanging SNAP benefits for cash and ineligible items; undercover operations observed illegal exchanges.
Bonheur's conviction may affect his immigration status; faces up to five years in prison and possible $250,000 fine.

title: JetBlue is cutting a route between Boston and this southern city
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue is discontinuing its seasonal route between Boston and Asheville, North Carolina, due to unmet performance expectations.
The route, launched in 2022, was intended to resume in May but will now cease operations.
Impacted customers will receive full refunds, while JetBlue refocuses on more sustainable routes.

title: Theater review: ‘Stereophonic’ at Emerson Colonial really makes you feel like you’re with the band
author: Jackie Chianca
bullets: 'Stereophonic' at Emerson Colonial Theatre runs from now through March 15.
The play features an immersive experience with original music by Will Butler of Arcade Fire.
Claire DeJean, Denver Milford, and Emilie Kouatchou lead a cast depicting musical recording dynamics.

title: Joe Mazzulla referenced his high school principal while defending Jaylen Brown after ejection
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected in the second quarter of the Celtics' loss to the Spurs after receiving two technical fouls.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla supported Brown, referencing his high school principal's decisiveness, criticizing the third referee's call.
The Celtics are set to play the defending NBA champion Thunder on Thursday in Oklahoma City at 9:30 p.m.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest movement announces March rally on Boston Common
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The 'No Kings' rally against authoritarianism is scheduled for March 28 at Boston Common from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m.
'No Kings' is organized by the ACLU of Massachusetts, Indivisible Mass Coalition, and Mass 50501.
The movement began in June 2025 with protests nationwide and aims to strengthen democracy in the U.S.

title: Now’s the time to book that Cape Cod getaway
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Cape Cod short-term rentals for July and August are 65% booked, urging travelers to secure accommodations soon.
The Forbes Travel Guide awarded Chatham Bars Inn on Cape Cod a five-star rating in 2026, highlighting its appeal.
Cape Cod has been named a top Memorial Day weekend destination by Conde Nast Traveler, boosting its popularity.

title: Charlie McAvoy’s reward as Bruins’ OT hero? Another trip to the dentist’s chair.
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Charlie McAvoy scored the OT winner in Bruins' 2-1 victory over the Kings, securing their 13th-straight home win.
McAvoy is battling dental injuries this season, requiring repeated visits to the dentist after taking multiple hits to the face.
Despite injuries, McAvoy has excelled, aiding Bruins' playoff push and having key roles in Team USA's recent Olympic success.

title: After her son was struck by a car, she’s running the Boston Marathon for Newton firefighters
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Maureen Murphy is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for the Newton Firefighters Children's Fund.
Murphy's son, Teddy, was struck by a car; Newton firefighters were the first responders providing medical care.
Murphy, from Newton, expresses gratitude and aims to support children of fallen firefighters through her marathon effort.

title: Victor Wembanyama shines, Jaylen Brown is ejected as Celtics drop chaotic loss to Spurs: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Victor Wembanyama scored 39 points with 11 rebounds, leading the Spurs to a 125-116 win over the Celtics.
Jaylen Brown was ejected early after a heated exchange with officials, impacting the Celtics' performance.
Derrick White excelled for Boston, scoring 34 points and performing well against Spurs star Wembanyama.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (March 11)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: In Abington, 353 Plymouth St., a 1,609 sq ft Colonial home, sold for $555,000 with 3 bedrooms and 1 bath.
In Andover, 4 Hazelwood Circle, a contemporary home with 5 bedrooms, sold for $1,778,500, occupying a lot over 39,000 sq ft.
Boston's 46 Chestnut St., a row-middle house with 6 bedrooms and 9 baths, sold for $22,000,000 spanning 6,682 sq ft.
